table-349-349.caption: Describe the information about rulings for card named 'Sublime Epiphany' with number 74s.
data: You must choose four *different* modes. You cannot choose the same mode multiple times.
Each chosen mode requires its own target(s) if applicable. All targets are chosen simultaneously.
The chosen modes resolve in the order they are printed on the card, from top to bottom.
The token copies the original creature as it exists on the battlefield, including any copy effects.
"Create a token that's a copy of target creature you control."
If a chosen target becomes illegal before resolution, that specific mode fails, but other modes still resolve.
If you control no creatures, you cannot choose the "create a token" or "gains hexproof" modes.
"Create a token...", "Target creature you control gains hexproof..."
The hexproof granted by Sublime Epiphany lasts until the end of the current turn.
"Target creature you control gains hexproof until end of turn."
